title: AI CEO Output Schema
description: Structured output produced by the AI CEO agent after evaluating a request and producing a governed execution plan.

planning_summary.description: A concise narrative summary of what the agent is planning to accomplish, the scope of work, and the key decisions made during planning. Should be human-readable and auditable.

assumptions.description: An explicit list of all assumptions made during planning. Each assumption should be stated clearly so reviewers can confirm or reject them before execution begins.

risks.description: Identified risks associated with the proposed execution path. Each risk entry should describe the risk, its likelihood, potential impact, and any mitigations built into the plan.

description.description: A clear description of the risk.

likelihood.description: Estimated likelihood that this risk materializes.

impact.description: Estimated impact if the risk materializes.

mitigation.description: Planned mitigation or contingency for this risk.

approval_requirements.description: List of explicit approval requirements that must be satisfied before or during execution. Each entry names the approval type, who must approve, and why it is required.

approval_type.description: The category or name of the required approval (e.g., 'human-in-the-loop', 'policy-gate', 'deployment-sign-off').

approver.description: The role, identity, or system responsible for granting this approval.

reason.description: Why this approval is required, referencing policy rules or risk factors.

blocking.description: Whether execution must halt until this approval is granted.

recommended_execution_path.description: The recommended execution strategy, including the ordered list of high-level phases, the skills to be involved, and the sequencing rationale.

phases.description: Ordered list of high-level execution phases.

phase_id.description: A short unique identifier for the phase (e.g., 'phase-1').

skills_involved.description: List of skills or sub-agents involved in this phase.

rationale.description: Explanation of why this execution path was chosen over alternatives, including any trade-offs considered.

verification_plan.description: The strategy for verifying that execution produced correct, safe results at each phase and overall.

checkpoints.description: Ordered list of verification checkpoints tied to execution phases.

after_phase.description: The phase_id after which this checkpoint is evaluated.

criteria.description: Specific, testable acceptance criteria for this checkpoint.

overall_acceptance_criteria.description: Top-level acceptance criteria that must all be satisfied for the full execution to be considered successful.

next_actions.description: The immediate next actions to be taken, in priority order. Each action includes the actor responsible and a clear description of what must happen.

action_id.description: A short unique identifier for this action.

actor.description: The agent, skill, or human role responsible for executing this action.

description.description: A clear, actionable description of what must be done.

depends_on.description: List of action_ids that must complete before this action can begin.
